project_overview.project_name: Agro-Climatic Resilience in Semi-Arid Landscapes (ACReSAL) Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To increase the implementation of sustainable landscape management practices in targeted watersheds in northern Nigeria and strengthen Nigeria's long-term enabling environment for integrated climate-resilient landscape management
project_overview.beneficiaries: 3.4 million direct project beneficiaries, including 1.68 million women; communities in 19 northern states and FCT dependent on natural resources; government institutions at federal and state levels

geographic_scope.coverage_type: Regional - 19 northern states and FCT covering approximately 55 million hectares of drylands
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: Yes - partial. Northern Nigeria experiences significant fragility and conflict, particularly in the northeast with Boko Haram and ISWAP insurgency, farmer-herder conflicts in the northwest, and banditry across large parts of the north. Approximately 2.2 million people displaced in the northeast; 8 million facing urgent humanitarian needs. Nigeria is on World Bank's list of fragile and conflict-affected situations.

key_components.description: Integrated watershed management planning and large-scale landscape investments including strategic watershed planning (20 watersheds), landscape investments (sand dune control, water resources management, agricultural strengthening, rangeland management), and special ecosystems management (wetlands, oases, protected areas, forests)

Community-level strengthening and investments including capacity building for sustainable natural resource management, micro-watershed planning, community-led landscape restoration (350,000 ha), climate-smart rainfed agriculture, and farmer-led irrigation development (FLID) with solar-powered systems

key_components.component_name: Component C: Institutional Strengthening and Project Management
key_components.description: Institutional and policy strengthening including monitoring infrastructure, knowledge platforms, data analytics systems, policy development, capacity building, and project management support at federal and state levels

key_components.component_name: Component D: Contingent Emergency Response Component (CERC)
key_components.description: Financing mechanism for quick deployment of uncommitted funds to respond to eligible crises or emergencies

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Federal Ministry of Environment (FMEnv) as lead implementing ministry, in collaboration with Federal Ministry of Water Resources (FMWR) and Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development (FMARD)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Federal Steering Committee (FSC) chaired by Minister of Environment, co-chaired by Ministers of Agriculture and Water Resources; Federal Technical Committee (FTC); State Steering Committees (SSC) and State Technical Committees (STC) in each participating state; Local Project Implementation Committees at community level; Memoranda of Understanding between FMEnv and other MDAs
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF; International open competitive procurement for major contracts; National open competitive procurement with World Bank requirements; Community-driven development procurement for Component B; Framework agreements for standard goods and services; e-Procurement systems in participating states; Prior review for high-risk contracts; Post review for lower-risk contracts
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Mixed-methods approach using ICT-enabled tools, GIS systems, remote sensing, UAVs, and field surveys; Management Information System (MIS) at federal and state levels; Semi-annual progress reports; Midterm review in year 3; Independent impact evaluation; Monitoring of conflict impacts; Results Framework with PDO and intermediate results indicators; Grievance Redress Mechanism; Third-party monitoring

identified_risks.risk_description: Political instability, changes in government priorities, weak inter-ministerial coordination, COVID-19 impacts on vulnerable populations
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong political support built at federal and state levels; Federal Steering Committee oversight; MOUs clarifying roles and responsibilities; Technical assistance through FPMU; Adaptive management approach

identified_risks.risk_description: Balance of payments constraints, limited government counterpart funding capacity, forex access limitations, economic slowdown from COVID-19
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project limits counterpart costs to land acquisition, resettlement, and staffing; Flexible disbursement arrangements; Coordination with SFTAS and ARMOR programs for fiscal support

identified_risks.risk_description: Overlapping mandates among multiple MDAs, gaps in mandates, weak policy coordination
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: MOUs between FMEnv and relevant MDAs; Federal and State Steering Committees for coordination; Technical assistance from FPMU; Lessons from NEWMAP implementation

identified_risks.risk_description: Innovative multisectoral approach may pose coordination challenges; Low disbursement in early years; Multiplicity of implementing agencies; Complex watershed management planning
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Shovel-ready technical designs available; Phased implementation approach; Expert advisors pool for quality assurance; Intensive supervision; Adaptive management; Lessons from NEWMAP, TRIMING, FADAMA

identified_risks.risk_description: Challenges in timely recruitment of technical specialists; Limited capacity in ESF implementation; Weak state-level implementation capacity
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Major investment in institutional strengthening (Subcomponent C1); Capacity building and training; Expert advisors; Implementation support missions; Competitive recruitment procedures; Lessons from NEWMAP

identified_risks.risk_description: Corruption and fraud risks; Weak contract management; Unethical conduct in procurement; Fake documents from bidders
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Robust FM arrangements through FPFMD and SPFMUs; Procurement audit and third-party monitoring; Spot checks on project sites; Statements of Ethical Conduct; Due diligence on bidder documents; Experienced engineering firms for supervision; Enhanced accountability framework; GRM; Social accountability mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: High environmental risk from widespread infrastructure investments; High social risk from labor influx, SEA/SH, land acquisition, resettlement, restrictions on resource access; Biodiversity impacts; Cumulative environmental impacts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF, RPF, LMP, IPMP, SEP, SMP, ESCP prepared and disclosed; Site-specific ESIAs and ESMPs before civil works; Biodiversity surveys and BMPs; GBV/SEA/SH action plan; Code of conduct for workers; GRM with SEA/SH protocols; E&S specialists in PMUs; Training on ESF; Community engagement; Survivor-centered approach to SEA/SH; Referral pathways to GBV services

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk of elite capture of project benefits; Exclusion of vulnerable groups; Weak community participation; Competing interests among stakeholders
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong culturally adapted communication plans; Qualified local NGOs for community mobilization; Participatory planning processes; Inclusive community structures; Specific targeting of vulnerable groups; Gender-focused investments; Grievance mechanism; Stakeholder Engagement Plan

identified_risks.risk_description: Extreme weather events (floods, droughts) may impact implementation; Climate variability may affect vegetation survival and project attribution; Heat waves may affect agriculture design
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: State-of-the-art land and restoration monitoring systems; Remote sensing and field monitoring; Adaptive management approach; Climate-smart agriculture technologies; Water storage and management investments; Flexible project design

identified_risks.risk_description: High security risk in many northern states from Boko Haram, ISWAP, banditry, kidnapping, farmer-herder conflicts; Risk to project staff, contractors, and beneficiaries; May limit implementation in some areas
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Security Risk Assessment conducted; Federal-level Security Management Plan (SMP) adopted as condition of effectiveness; State-level SMPs as disbursement conditions; Regular SMP updates; Security training for project staff; Reporting mechanisms for security changes; Adaptive project implementation; Strong FGN and state commitment to security; Third-party monitoring; Remote supervision capabilities

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: High Risk (Environmental and Social Risk Classification)
triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS5: Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement
ESS6: Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources
ESS10: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Project-based GRM developed and implemented by FPMU with provisions for access at state and local levels. Includes reporting channels for SEA/SH cases that are ethical, confidential, and safe for women and girls. Operators trained on survivor-centered approach. Referral pathways to health, legal, safety, and psychosocial support services. Procedural Manual for grievance redress officers. Grievance redress procedures at various levels with women representatives. World Bank's Grievance Redress Service (GRS) also available.
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Significant gender focus: Target of 1.68 million female direct beneficiaries (50% of 3.4 million); Minimum 20% of revolving funds awarded to women-led/owned enterprises; CRF management committees required to have at least one female executive; Preferential scoring for subprojects with women beneficiaries; Engagement of more female extension agents; Gender-responsive approach to irrigation development; Women's participation in community structures and leadership positions; Addressing gender inequalities in agricultural productivity and access to inputs; Support for women's livelihood diversification; Reduction of GBV through service provider mapping, referral pathways, training, and social marketing campaigns

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project explicitly designed with conflict sensitivity: Micro-watershed plans include mapping of conflicts and conflict resolution mechanisms; Community strengthening includes peace building and conflict resolution capacity building; Joint planning and implementation across stakeholder groups to foster mutual understanding; Investments in social cohesion and peacebuilding; Monitoring of conflict impacts in project area through aggregation and analysis of community data; Addresses root causes of conflict through improved natural resource management and livelihood diversification; Farmer-herder conflict mitigation through improved rangeland management, water availability, and pastoral support; Community-based decision-making for natural resource management; Inclusive participation of marginalized groups to reduce tensions
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Northern Nigeria faces multiple fragility drivers: Armed insurgency (Boko Haram, ISWAP) in northeast with decade-long conflict; Farmer-herder conflicts over grazing land and water access causing 10,000+ deaths 2011-2018; Banditry and armed violence across large parts of north; Approximately 2.2 million IDPs in northeast; 8 million people facing urgent humanitarian needs; Climate change and desertification exacerbate resource scarcity and competition; Land degradation reduces agricultural productivity and increases poverty; Water scarcity drives migration and conflict; Weak state capacity and governance; High poverty rates (exceeding 80% in some states); Limited livelihood opportunities; Weak social cohesion in conflict-affected areas
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: High security risk acknowledged: Security Risk Assessment conducted for all participating states; Federal-level Security Management Plan (SMP) adopted as condition of effectiveness; State-level SMPs required as disbursement conditions; SMPs to be constantly updated to respond to changes in security situation; Project staff to receive security training including mechanisms for reporting security context changes; Project management to consider adaptations in implementation based on security feedback; Remote supervision systems to support implementation in high-risk areas; Third-party monitoring of project implementation; Flexibility in project design to adapt to security constraints; Coordination with FGN and state governments on security measures; Potential for reduced participation in some areas due to security constraints
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: Yes - significant displacement context: Approximately 2.2 million IDPs in northeast Nigeria; Project area includes conflict-affected populations; Resettlement Policy Framework (RPF) prepared to address potential involuntary resettlement and land acquisition; No voluntary land donations expected; Where restrictions on resource access occur, compensation provided in line with ESS5; Potential for land acquisition for water resources management investments; Complex land tenure situations in northern Nigeria affecting IDPs, women, pastoralists, and minorities; IDPs have limited access to land due to financial constraints and instability; Project includes support for IDP reintegration and livelihood support; Internally displaced persons specifically targeted as vulnerable group for project benefits
